task.title: Preserve broken pipeline-head layout in multiline blocks
task.summary: The formatter now repairs several tight continuation issues, but it still sometimes over-compacts the head of a pipeline even when the surrounding pipeline is intentionally multiline.
task.problem: In `lima-ports.nu`, a multiline pipeline inside the `else` branch currently reforms from: `ls $lima_dir` `| where type == dir` into `ls $lima_dir | where type == dir` while the remaining stages stay on later lines. That creates an inconsistent shape where only the first stage collapses upward. This is not the same problem as restoring a broken comparison continuation; it is a layout decision about whether a pipeline that is already broken across lines should keep its head on its own line.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: When a pipeline is already multiline and subsequent stages remain on later lines, the formatter preserves the pipeline head on its own line instead of collapsing only the first stage upward.
The formatter still restores genuinely broken operator continuations like `where type` followed by `== dir` when that is the actual residue.
Regression coverage is drawn from the real `lima-ports.nu` fixture shape.
